key_insight: Gradient detonation-sprayed NiCr–Al coatings outperform homogeneous coatings by maintaining a more protective oxide layer and slowing high-temperature oxidation.
engineering_relevance: For thermal power plant components, coating architecture matters: compositional grading can improve durability, reduce spallation, and extend service life in severe hot, corrosive environments.
method: Detonation-sprayed gradient and homogeneous NiCr–Al composite coatings
Real operating-condition high-temperature oxidation testing in a thermal power plant
SEM, phase analysis, and cross-sectional elemental redistribution/oxide-scale assessment
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that gradient coatings preserved structure better, showed more uniform Al/Cr distribution, and formed more continuous Al2O3/Cr2O3 scales than homogeneous coatings under prolonged exposure.
watch_item: Results are based on one coating system and service environment, so transferability to other substrates, temperatures, and duty cycles should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Investigation of Detonation-Sprayed NiCr–Al Coatings Tested under Real Operating Conditions of a Thermal Power Plant
